title: Spatchcock Grilled Chicken
description: Whole chicken spatchcocked, rubbed with avocado oil and Herbs de Provence, grilled at 400–425°F with the lid closed and turned every 15 minutes. Crisp skin, juicy meat throughout.

instructions.text: Spatchcock the chicken: place breast-side down on a cutting board. Using kitchen shears, cut along both sides of the backbone and remove it. Flip the chicken breast-side up and press firmly on the breastbone until it cracks flat. Pat completely dry with paper towels.

Rub the chicken all over with avocado oil. Combine salt, pepper, and Herbs de Provence and rub evenly over both sides, getting under the skin where possible. Let rest at room temperature while the grill preheats.
Preheat grill to 400–425°F with lid closed. Set up for indirect heat -- if using a 3-burner grill, turn off the center burner; on a 2-burner, turn one side to low.
Place chicken skin-side up over indirect heat, lid closed. Cook 15 minutes.
Flip chicken. Cook another 15 minutes lid closed.
Flip again. Cook a final 10–15 minutes. Check internal temp — 165°F in the thickest part of the thigh, away from bone.
Rest the chicken 10 minutes before carving. The rest is not optional — it keeps the juices in the meat.

notes:
Spatchcocking flattens the bird so it cooks more evenly and faster than a whole roast chicken. The skin gets crispier too since more surface area is exposed.

If the skin is getting too dark before the meat is done, move the chicken to a cooler part of the grill or tent loosely with foil.

Pairs naturally with roasted fingerling potatoes and grilled asparagus -- start the potatoes in the oven before the chicken goes on the grill.
